title: rhinanthus × poeverleinii
intro: Rhinanthus × poeverleinii, also known as Poeverlein's Rampion or Poison Rampion, is a rare, hybrid plant species within the Orobanchaceae family that has gained attention for its unique properties. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to act as a purgative and a diuretic, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The health benefits associated with Rhinanthus × poeverleinii include its potential to treat digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. In herbal preparations, Rhinanthus × poeverleinii is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to take advantage of its medicinal properties, although it is essential to note that excessive consumption can be toxic.
